mission.title: Install and Configure Opentelemetry on Kubernetes
mission.description: OpenTelemetry is a set of APIs, libraries, agents, and instrumentation to provide observability for applications. Installing OpenTelemetry allows you to collect and export telemetry data from your applications, helping you monitor performance and troubleshoot issues.

steps.title: Add the OpenTelemetry Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the OpenTelemetry Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add open-telemetry https://open-telemetry.github.io/opentelemetry-helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install OpenTelemetry Collector
steps.description:
Install the OpenTelemetry Collector using Helm. This command will create a new namespace called 'observability' and deploy the collector there.
```bash
helm install opentelemetry-collector open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector --namespace observability --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the OpenTelemetry Collector pod is running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace observability
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Update the OpenTelemetry Collector deployment to set resource limits. This command modifies the existing deployment to add CPU and memory limits.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment opentelemetry-collector --namespace observability --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources", "value": {"limits": {"cpu": "500m", "memory": "512Mi"}, "requests": {"cpu": "250m", "memory": "256Mi"}}}]'
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access the Collector
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access the OpenTelemetry Collector's endpoint for testing.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/opentelemetry-collector --namespace observability 55680:55680
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the OpenTelemetry Collector running in the 'observability' namespace, and you will be able to access its endpoint through port forwarding. You should also see the resource limits applied to the deployment.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add open-telemetry https://open-telemetry.github.io/opentelemetry-helm-charts
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
